Args:

    seqparams: Parameters to be bound into the SQL statement.
    parameters: Parameters to be set the SQL statement.

Returns:
    The cursor itself, or None if some error happened.

functions.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.utils.normalize_file_path
documentation:
Normalize a file path by removing the file:/, file://, or file:/// protocol if present.
Preserves the leading slash and returns the result as a raw string.

Args:
file_url (str): The file path or URL to normalize.

Returns:
str: The normalized local file path as a raw string.

modules.name: clickzetta.connector.v0._helpers
documentation: Shared helper functions for ClickZetta API classes.

modules.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions
documentation: Exceptions used in the ClickZetta DB-API.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.Warning
documentation: Exception raised for important DB-API warnings.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.Error
documentation: Exception representing all non-warning DB-API errors.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.InterfaceError
documentation: DB-API error related to the database interface.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.DatabaseError
documentation: DB-API error related to the database.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.DataError
documentation: DB-API error due to problems with the processed data.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.OperationalError
documentation:
DB-API error related to the database operation.

These errors are not necessarily under the control of the programmer.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.IntegrityError
documentation: DB-API error when integrity of the database is affected.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.InternalError
documentation: DB-API error when the database encounters an internal error.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.ProgrammingError
documentation: DB-API exception raised for programming errors.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.exceptions.NotSupportedError
documentation: DB-API error for operations not supported by the database or API.

documentation:
Converts Python data to Clickzetta data for pyformat/format style.

The output is bound in a query in the client side.

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.connection.Connection.commit
documentation: No-op, but for consistency raise an error if connection is closed.

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.connection.Connection._process_params_qmarks
documentation:
Process parameters for client-side parameter binding.

Args:
    params: Either a sequence, or a dictionary of parameters, if anything else
        is given then it will be put into a list and processed that way.

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.v0.connection.Connection._process_single_param
documentation:
Process a single parameter to Clickzetta understandable form.

This is a convenience function to replace repeated multiple calls with a single
function call.

It calls the following underlying functions in this order:
    1. self.converter.convert_to
    2. self.converter.quote

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.adjust_engine_params
documentation: Return a new URL and `connect_args` for a specific catalog/schema.

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.execute
documentation:
Execute a SQL query

```
param cursor

Cursor instance

param query

Query to execute

param kwargs

kwargs to be passed to cursor.execute()

return

```

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.execute_with_cursor
documentation:
Trigger execution of a query and handle the resulting cursor.

For most implementations this just makes calls to `execute` and
`handle_cursor` consecutively, but in some engines (e.g. Trino) we may
need to handle client limitations such as lack of async support and
perform a more complicated operation to get information from the cursor
in a timely manner and facilitate operations such as query stop

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.get_function_names
documentation:
Get a list of function names that are able to be called on the database.
Used for SQL Lab autocomplete.

```
param database

The database to get functions for

return

A list of function names useable in the database
```

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.is_readonly_query
documentation: Pessimistic readonly, 100% sure statement won't mutate anything

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.get_extra_params
documentation:
Add a user agent to be used in the requests.
Trim whitespace from connect_args to avoid databricks driver errors

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.get_cancel_query_id
documentation:
Select identifiers from the database engine that uniquely identifies the
queries to cancel. The identifier is typically a session id, process id
or similar.

```
param cursor

Cursor instance in which the query will be executed

param query

Query instance

return

Query identifier
```

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.cancel_query
documentation:
Cancel query in the underlying database.

```
param cursor

New cursor instance to the db of the query

param query

Query instance

param cancel_query_id

ClickZetta job id

return

True if query cancelled successfully, False otherwise
```

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.get_schema_from_engine_params
documentation:
Return the configured schema.

For Clickzetta the SQLAlchemy URI looks like this:

    clickzetta://user:password@instance_name.host/workspace[?schema=schema_name&key=value...]

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec._extract_partition_info_from_df
documentation: Clickzetta partitions look like ds={partition name}/ds={partition name}

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.latest_partition
documentation:
Returns col name and the latest (max) partition value for a table

```
param table_name

the name of the table

param schema

schema / database / namespace

param database

database query will be run against

type database

models.Database

param show_first

displays the value for the first partitioning key
if there are many partitioning keys

param indexes

indexes from the database

type show_first

bool
```

```pycon
>>> latest_partition('foo_table')
(['ds'], ('2018-01-01',))
```

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.get_url_for_impersonation
documentation:
Return a modified URL with the username set.

```
param url

SQLAlchemy URL object

param impersonate_user

Flag indicating if impersonation is enabled

param username

Effective username
```

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.superset.clickzetta.ClickZettaEngineSpec.get_view_names
documentation:
Get all the view names within the specified schema.

Per the SQLAlchemy definition if the schema is omitted the database’s default
schema is used, however some dialects infer the request as schema agnostic.

```
param database

The database to inspect

param inspector

The SQLAlchemy inspector

param schema

The schema to inspect

returns

The view names
```

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.sqlalchemy.datatype.TIMESTAMP_LTZ

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.sqlalchemy.datatype.TIMESTAMP_LTZ.__init__
documentation:
Construct a new {class}`datatype.TIMESTAMP_LTZ`.

```
param timezone

boolean.  Indicates that the TIMESTAMP_LTZ type should
enable timezone support, if available on the target database.
On a per-dialect basis is similar to "TIMESTAMP WITH TIMEZONE".
If the target database does not support timezones, this flag is
ignored.
```

The SQL TIMESTAMP_LTZ type.

{class}`datatype.TIMESTAMP_LTZ` datatypes have support for timezone
storage on some backends, such as PostgreSQL and Oracle. Use the
{paramref}`~types.TIMESTAMP.timezone` argument in order to enable
"TIMESTAMP WITH TIMEZONE" for these backends.

classes.name: clickzetta.connector.sqlalchemy.datatype.TIMESTAMP_NTZ

methods.name: clickzetta.connector.sqlalchemy.datatype.TIMESTAMP_NTZ.__init__
documentation:
Construct a new {class}`datatype.TIMESTAMP_NTZ`.

```
param timezone

boolean.  Indicates that the TIMESTAMP_NTZ type should
enable timezone support, if available on the target database.
On a per-dialect basis is similar to "TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIMEZONE".
If the target database does not support timezones, this flag is
ignored.
```

The SQL TIMESTAMP_NTZ type.

{class}`datatype.TIMESTAMP_NTZ` datatypes have support for timezone
storage on some backends, such as PostgreSQL and Oracle. Use the
{paramref}`~types.TIMESTAMP.timezone` argument in order to enable
"TIMESTAMP WITH TIMEZONE" for these backends.
